item.title: Footballers and family lay flowers at funeral of Diogo Jota and André Silva
item.content:encoded: Jota, 28, was laid to rest alongside his brother, Silva, 25, after they died in a car crash on Thursday.

item.title: Dozens dead after flash floods - Did the early warning systems fail? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Officials say that at least 27 people have been killed by severe flash flooding in the US state of Texas. Rescue teams are searching for more than 20 girls from a summer camp who are still unaccounted for. The floods swept through south-central Texas, hitting Kerr County, which lies on the Guadalupe River. Residents were caught off guard when a month's worth of rain fell in just a few hours.
Chapters: 0:00 At least 27 dead, many missing in Texas flash flooding 0:25 John Downey, News anchor at KTRH radio 1:39 Jess Neumann, Associate Prof. of Hydrology at the University of Reading
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: Evidence of needle attacks at French festival hard to find | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
About 145 people, mostly young women, reported they were pricked with syringes during Fete de la Musique, a music festival across France. Now a French newspaper reports that there's no evidence for the spike attacks and experts say it's hard to prove. But people on social media have recounted how they experienced it.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: Protests in Mexico City against gentrification, rising rent prices and digital nomads | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Tourists were shouted at and store windows smashed during a protest against gentrification in Mexico City, where an influx of Americans and other "digital nomads" is driving up rents and pushing out locals.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews

item.title: US aid workers wounded, says Gaza Humanitarian Foundation
item.content:encoded: Militants threw grenades at an aid centre in Khan Younis, the controversial relief organisation says.

item.title: Dry wind threatens to bring new wildfires to Crete | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Authorities in Greece say a massive wildfire on the island of Crete is mostly contained. The blaze broke out on Wednesday and quickly spread to the island's southeastern coast. Officials say some 5,000 residents and tourists were forced to evacuate their homes. But with dry windy weather conditions continuing, the threat may not be over yet. DW's Sofia Kleftaki reports from Heraklion.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1
#Greece #Crete #Wildfires

item.title: Diogo Jota and André Silva's funeral held in Portugal
item.content:encoded: The community in Gondomar grieves after the deaths of Liverpool star Diogo Jota and his brother, André Silva.

item.title: Flash flood catches Texas off guard, rescuers search for survivors | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Officials say flash flooding in the US state of Texas has killed at least 24 people. Rescue teams are searching for more than 20 girls from a summer camp who are unaccounted for. The floods swept through south-central Texas, hitting Kerr County, which lies on the Guadalupe river. Residents were caught off-guard as a month's worth of rain came down in just a few hours.
00:00 Flash flood hits Texas off guard 03:07 DW speaks with John Downey, news anchor at KTRH Radio in Houston
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Deadly floods in texas | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Deadly floods have already killed dozens of people in Texas's Kerr County after unexpectedly heavy rains hit the region on July 4.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Hamas ready to start ceasefire talks 'immediately' as Trump, Netanyahu set to meet | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Hamas says it's ready to begin talks immediately on a US-backed proposal for a 60-day ceasefire in Gaza. The plan would involve a phased release of hostages and a significant increase in humanitarian aid to the territory. US President Donald Trump said he's optimistic and that there could be a deal next week. He's been pushing both sides for an end to the war. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is due to travel to Washington for talks on Monday.
00:00 Hamas agrees to ceasefire 00:30 DW speaks with journalist Karim El-Gawhary in Cairo
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1
#Gaza #Israel #IsraelHamasWar

item.title: Australian PM vows 'full force of law' after arson attack at synagogue
item.content:encoded: “Antisemitism has no place in Australia,” Anthony Albanese said after the latest in a string of attacks.

item.title: Ukraine braces for Russian offensive near Sumy amid drone attacks on cities | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Russian drone attacks have struck several parts of Ukraine overnight, including residential buildings in the southern region of Kherson. This comes as crews are still clearing debris from drone and missile attacks that battered the capital Kyiv in the biggest aerial bombardment since the start of the war. In his nightly video address, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he and US President Donald Trump had agreed to work on increasing Kyiv's air defenses. On Friday, Trump spoke to reporters on Air Force One about Ukraine, including the potential of sending more Patriot missile systems. Meanwhile, Russian forces are closing in on Ukraine's Sumy region near Russia's border. And Ukraine is trying to hold them back. Defense lines are being strengthened as tens of thousands of Russian troops advance towards the strategic city.
00:00 Russian drones strike Ukraine overnight 01:00 DW speaks with Frank Ledwidge, former UK military intelligence officer and current senior lecturer in strategic studies at Portsmouth University 04:47 Ukraine braces for Russian offensive in Sumy
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1
#Ukraine #Russia #UkraineWar

item.title: River Seine reopens to swimmers in Paris after century-long ban
item.content:encoded: For the first time in more than 100 years people will be able to swim legally in three sections of the river.

item.title: Watch: Holiday park wiped out by Texas floods
item.content:encoded: Jason Allen, correspondent at the BBC's US partner CBS, reports from Kerrville, Texas.

item.title: Australian actor Julian McMahon dies aged 56
item.content:encoded: "Julian loved life," said the wife of the actor best known for roles in Nip/Tuck, FBI: Most Wanted and Fantastic Four.

item.title: Greece: From Economic Outcast to Euro-Area Outperformer
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Ten years ago, Greece had already spent five years cut off from financial markets, its economy had lost a quarter of its output and society was enduring unprecedented austerity. Today, the nation is outperforming its euro-zone peers on several fronts, growing faster than the European average and achieving budget surpluses. Bloomberg's Paul Tugwell discusses how much changed in the last decade for Greece. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
Like this video? Subscribe and turn on notifications so you don't miss any videos from Bloomberg Markets & Finance: https://tinyurl.com/ysu5b8a9 Visit http://www.bloomberg.com for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more.

item.title: China or Tibetans: how will the next Dalai Lama be chosen? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
China has condemned the Dalai Lama ahead of his 90th birthday for asserting that Tibetans in exile will choose his successor. Beijing, which labels him a "separatist," insists it will decide who the next Dalai Lama is. Tibetan Buddhists believe the Dalai Lama is reincarnated and identified through spiritual practices. China, however, plans to appoint the next one itself. The Chinese Communist Party has tightly controlled Tibet since its 1950 takeover, leading to the Dalai Lama’s exile in 1959 and ongoing repression of Tibetan Buddhism. Surveillance, military buildup, and bans on his image aim to suppress dissent. This is the latest episode of DW’s explainer series Inside Asian Conflicts.
#InsideAsianConflicts #tibet #dalailama
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: South African Agriculture Braces for Trump Tariffs
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
US President Donald Trump's de-escalation on tariffs offered a temporary reprieve to African businesses, some which initially considered abandoning the US market. In South Africa, the world's second biggest citrus exporter, uncertainty remains over whether Trump will ultimately impose a 30% levy or stick to his baseline 10%. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja visited a producer ramping up shipments before Trump's 90-day postponement of his higher rates expires.
Watch the full episode: https://youtu.be/NvDCrJ1PTU0 -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Watch: Deadly Texas flooding causes destruction
item.content:encoded: Dozens of people are missing as flash floods sweep away mobile homes, vehicles and holiday cabins.

item.title: Watch: Deadly Texas flooding causes destruction
item.content:encoded: Dozens of people are missing as flash floods sweep away mobile homes, vehicles and holiday cabins.

item.title: French lawmakers aim to curb the growing fast fashion market | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Twelve million duty-free parcels arrive in the EU from China every day. Proof of the rapid growth in online fast fashion traders. Now, France is planning a law to challenge disposable clothing. Fast fashion is coming under growing criticism – and not just on the environmental front.
#DWBusiness
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews

item.title: Nuclear watchdog exits Iran; Hamas positive about ceasefire deal, and more
How mediators might bridge Israel and Hamas demands | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Hamas says it's ready to begin talks immediately on a US-backed proposal for a 60-day ceasefire in Gaza. The plan is expected to involve a phased release of the remaining hostages and a significant increase in humanitarian aid to the strip. US President Donald Trump says Israel has already accepted the terms. But Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has yet to comment on the proposal. 00:00 Hamas ready to start ceasefire talks 'immediately' 02:27 Giorgio Cafiero, Gulf States Analytics
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: How the Success of Sonic the Hedgehog is Reviving Japan's Sega
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
President & COO of Sega Corporation, Shuji Utsumi, says the video game company is teaming up with international partners to expand in Asia and lower its reliance on the Japanese market. Utsumi also says he was impressed with Nintendo Switch 2 sales and hopes the trend continues" to boost business for the sector. He speaks with Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin from Kyoto. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: What could be behind the intensified attacks? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has spoken with Donald Trump about strengthening Ukraine's air defenses. Hundreds of Russian drones and missiles have battered the capital Kyiv in the biggest aerial bombardment since the start of the war. Russia has been stepping up its attacks in recent weeks, as the US has halted supplies of some defensive equipment to Ukraine.
00:00 Russia launches massive drone attack 02:16 Jan-Philipp Scholz, DW Correspondent 04:46 Marina Miron, King's College London 09:38 Artis Pabriks, Northern Defense Policy Centre
#russia #ukraine # For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.author: RSS-Bridge
item.content:encoded: https://www.reuters.com/pf/api/v3/content/fetch/articles-by-section-alias-or-id-v1?query={"section_id":"\/home","size":30,"website":"reuters"} resulted in 401 Unauthorized #0 index.php(72): RssBridge->main() #1 lib/RssBridge.php(103): DisplayAction->execute() #2 actions/DisplayAction.php(68): DisplayAction->createResponse() #3 actions/DisplayAction.php(117): ReutersBridge->collectData() #4 bridges/ReutersBridge.php(500): getContents() #5 lib/contents.php(110): HttpException::fromResponse() action=display&bridge=ReutersBridge&feed=home/topnews&format=Atom 2024-02-02 (git.master.257799b) Go back

item.title: How Trump bill could cede US global market shares to China | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Donald Trump has signed his "Big Beautiful Bill" into law, a day after it was passed by the US Congress with overwhelming Republican support. The president formally approved the legislation at the White House during the 4th of July celebrations. The bill enacts key parts of Trump's agenda including cutting taxes, and boosting spending on defense and immigration control. Democrats and some Republicans criticized sweeping cuts to federal safety net programs, warning millions could lose health coverage. Trump defended the bill - one of the most significant victories so far of his second term.
00:00 Trump bill boosts fossil fuel 01:06 Tyson Barker, Political analyst 09:07 Protesters share concerns over Trump bill 11:40 Liz Schuelke, Policy analyst
#us #trump #economy For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: Trump Signs 'Big, Beautiful Bill' at July 4th Ceremony
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump signed his $3.4 trillion budget bill into law Friday, enshrining an extension of tax cuts, temporary new breaks for tipped workers and funding to crack down on illegal immigration. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Trump Speaks on US Stock Market and Jobs
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump spoke at the White House, kicking off 4th off July celebrations. He said the stock market and jobs are at an all-time high and that "we're going to keep it that way." -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Tennis hero Arthur Ashe's South African legacy: 'The first free black man I'd ever seen'
item.content:encoded: The tennis champion wanted to be remembered for fighting racism, not just his sporting prowess.

item.title: 'Do they have gold in them?': The Indian artisans up in arms over Prada's sandals
item.content:encoded: Artisans who make Kolhapuri sandals are demanding better recognition after Prada failed to acknowledge design roots.

item.title: Tiny creatures gorge, get fat, and help fight global warming
item.content:encoded: Scientists find out how the epic deep sea migration of a tiny animal is storing planet-warming carbon.

item.title: Trump signs sweeping tax and spending bill into law
item.content:encoded: A signing ceremony at the White House on Friday afternoon kicked off celebrations for 4 July.

item.title: What's behind Russia's recognition of the Taliban government? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Russia has become the first country to officially recognize Afghanistan's Taliban government as the country's legitimate authority. This marks a significant step towards international acceptance of the Taliban nearly four years after they took back control of Afghanistan.
00:00 Russia first country to recognize Taliban gov't 03:16 Alexey Yusupov, Russia policy analyst 09:34 Ibraheem Bahiss, International Crisis Group
#russia #taliban #afghanistan For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Dog saves owners from russian drone attack | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Buddy the dog saved his family from a Russian air attack: "If it weren't for him, we would have died."
#dwnewsshorts For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: US debt is now $37tn – should we be worried?
item.content:encoded: President Trump's budget bill will add to the US debt pile but is there a limit to how much the rest of the world will lend Uncle Sam?

item.title: Watch: Moment Oasis take to the stage for first gig in 16 years
item.content:encoded: The Gallagher brothers play their first concert since reuniting, in front of a packed Cardiff stadium.

item.title: Residential building collapses in Karachi
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
A residential building in Karachi, which had been previously condemned, collapsed, killing at least six people in one of the city's poorest neighborhoods. The latest death toll stands at eight victims, with several wounded and more believed to be trapped in the rubble. Pakistani's president, Asif Ali Zardari, ordered an urgent probe into the causes of the incident and called for effective measures to be taken to prevent similar events in the future, according to a statement by his Pakistan People's Party (PPP).
#dwnewsshorts
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch

item.title: Why Tibet matters | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
China says it must have the final say on choosing the next spiritual leader of Tibet. The current Dala Lama sys his successor will likely be born outside of China. This religious controversy is deeply political. While few contest that Tibet is part of China, how Beijing deals with Tibetans and Tibet could have a huge impact on the rest of Asia. Most of Asia’s major rivers start on the Tibetan plateau.
#dwnewsshorts #dalailama #InsideAsianConflicts
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Hamas says it delivered 'positive response' on US Gaza ceasefire plan
item.content:encoded: The group says it is ready to begin negotiations immediately, as Donald Trump says there can possibly be a deal next week.

item.title: American subcontractors at GHF aid distribution sites in Gaza have voiced their concerns | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Footage and anonymous testimony obtained by the Associated Press suggest that US-contracted security personnel at aid distribution sites in Gaza have used force near civilians, including stun grenades and live ammunition. The Gaza Humanitarian Foundation, a US and Israeli-backed aid program, is facing criticism from rights groups and aid organizations over its operations and alleged risks to civilians. The organization denies any serious incidents. Access to the sites remains restricted, making independent verification difficult.
#dwnewsshorts
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Ancient Benin bronzes get a welcome ceremony as they return to Nigeria | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Nigeria receives the largest batch yet of ancient Benin artefacts from abroad. DW correspondent Olisa Chukwumah reports from the ceremony in Lagos where a Dutch museum handed over the items back to Nigeria.
#benin #nigeria #africanews
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Notorious Swedish gang leader arrested in Turkey
item.content:encoded: Ismail Abdo, the leader of the Rumba crime gang, has been at the centre of a violent turf war with a rival gang in Sweden.

item.title: Notorious Swedish gang leader arrested in Turkey
item.content:encoded: Ismail Abdo, the leader of the Rumba crime gang, has been at the centre of a violent turf war with a rival gang in Sweden.

item.title: What's next for Thailand? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
DW correspondent Georg Matthes looks at the state of Thai politics. What options does the troubled kingdom have, and how likely is another military coup?
#dwnewsshorts #thailand
